stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Selective Laser Sintering (SLS) is a form of additive manufacturing that uses a laser to fuse powdered material into 3D objects.
  • Step 1: A thin layer of powdered nylon is spread across a build platform.
  • Step 2: A laser traces the cross-section of the object, fusing powder where the laser strikes.
  • Step 3: The build platform lowers, and a fresh layer of powder is spread on top.
  • Step 4: After the part is complete, it is removed from the powder bed and brushed clean for inspection.
stem: The student wants to describe how SLS produces 3D objects. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: SLS is a form of additive manufacturing that creates a powder bed in which a laser fuses material layer by layer before being brushed clean, creating a high-quality 3D object.
SLS produces 3D objects by passing a laser through a powder bed, adding layers to the object, then brushing it in a final step.
In SLS, a laser fuses powdered nylon layer by layer onto a build platform that lowers between layers; once complete, the part is removed and brushed clean.
In SLS, a laser melts layers of powdered nylon until the part solidifies and passes through a fresh layer, wherein the build is finished.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer because it gives a clear, ordered description of the SLS process: laser fusion, platform lowering, completion, and cleaning.

Choices A, B, and D either compress the steps incorrectly or distort the process.
